applicability_notes: constraints: {'input_layout': 'TILE', 'index_layout': 'TILE', 'index_dtype': ['uint16', 'uint32'], 'input_dtype': ['bfloat16', 'float32'], 'output_shape': 'matches index.shape', 'notes': 'index must be expanded/repeated to full gather output shape before call (see call sites using ttnn.reshape + ttnn.repeat + ttnn.to_layout(TILE) on index); index dtype must be unsigned int (typecast to uint32 observed at attention call sites)'}; fusion_notes: Frequently preceded by index-preparation chain: ttnn.reshape(idx) -> ttnn.repeat(idx, broadcast_dims) -> ttnn.to_layout(idx, TILE_LAYOUT) [-> ttnn.typecast(idx, uint32)] -> ttnn.gather. This whole chain corresponds to a single torch.gather with broadcastable index or torch index_select-style selection (e.g. top-k token/expert selection: topk indices -> gather scores/keys/queries).

placement_observations.source: unit tests: ttnn.from_torch(input, ttnn.bfloat16, layout=ttnn.Layout.TILE, device=device) with no explicit memory_config (default DRAM interleaved)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same rank as input, gather dim may be smaller (e.g. [1,1,512,32] vs input [1,1,512,64])
placement_observations.source: unit tests: ttnn.from_torch(index, ttnn.uint16/uint32, layout=ttnn.Layout.TILE, device=device), no explicit memory_config
unit tests: preallocated out tensor created via ttnn.from_torch(output, ttnn.bfloat16, layout=TILE, device=device), no explicit memory_config

placement_observations.size_descriptor: [batch, n_heads, top_u/sample_k, head_dim] after reshape+repeat broadcast
placement_observations.source: model call sites: sparse attention query/key sampling — sample_idx/q_idx/mask_idx prepared with reshape→repeat→to_layout(TILE)→typecast(uint32) then ttnn.gather(kh/qh/attn_mask, dim=2, index=...)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: scores_flat [B,1,S,num_experts], gather on dim=3 with topk indices
placement_observations.source: model call site: MoE router — ttnn.gather(scores_flat, dim=3, index=topk_experts_indices) followed by sum/div normalization; inputs deallocated immediately after, indicating DRAM interleaved working set
